raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Evoking a stance of calibrated courage, where fear is acknowledged yet subordinated to evidence-based hope in a self-affirming, almost incantatory register.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Fear and evidence can be weighed against each other as commensurable guides to action",
    "The speaker possesses or can access 'evidence' reliable enough to override visceral aversion",
    "Willingness itself is a stable trait rather than a fluctuating state",
    "Actions that 'help' are identifiable in advance through rational assessment"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally consistent within its figurative frame; it elegantly juxtaposes emotional resistance and epistemic trust without claiming either is absolute.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The listener or reader treats the line as an aspirational self-description that encourages reflective risk-taking when data genuinely favors benefit, and when fear is not itself a valuable signal.",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "The statement is taken to license overriding all fear on the basis of incomplete or self-serving 'evidence,' or when it masks an inability to distinguish signal from noise in one's own emotional responses."
}
